title: Read Transit Schedules
summary: Decode bus and train schedules so you know when and where to go.
learnerPromise: Decode bus and train schedules so you know when and where to go.
whyItMatters: Public transit is often the cheapest way to get around. Reading schedules well means less waiting and more control over your time.
realLifeUses: Catch the right bus to school or work.
Plan a train trip with a connection at a transfer station.
Figure out weekend or holiday service changes.
Help a friend navigate transit in your city.
youWillLearn: Read a timetable and find departure times for your stop.
Understand route numbers, direction labels, and terminal names.
Find platform, gate, or boarding location info.
Spot service exceptions like holidays or rush-hour only routes.
miniChallenge: Find a bus or train schedule online and plan one trip using it.
steps: Find the timetable for your route — paper, app, or website.
Identify your stop and the direction you are traveling.
Find the next departure time that fits your schedule.
Note the platform or stop location and any transfers needed.
Arrive at the stop a few minutes early. Board and confirm you are on the right route.
completionCriteria: You can read a transit timetable and pick the correct departure time for your stop.
You can identify route numbers, directions, and transfer points.
commonProblems: You are looking at the wrong direction — timetables are split by inbound and outbound.
Weekend schedules differ from weekday — check the day of the week.
The route has multiple variants — match the destination or terminal name, not just the number.
tips: Have a backup route in case you miss your connection.
Use a transit app with real-time updates when available.
Snap a photo of the timetable so you have it offline.
